mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates month-over-month, signaling strengthening labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives equity valuations upward through increased earnings expectations and reduced recession risk over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2009 recovery phase with employment stabilization
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Payroll growth acceleration does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains at or near zero while inflation (CPI) remains subdued, indicating sustained monetary accommodation. This low real rate environment compresses equity risk premiums and supports forward equity returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:December 2009 FEDFUNDS at 5.33 bps, CPIAUCSL at 313.225
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with price stability
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline sharply month-over-month, indicating weakening demand for residential construction and reduced forward economic activity. This signals deteriorating demand conditions that compress equity multiples over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1360.0 thousand units in October 2009
regime_hint: demand contraction or housing market weakness
failure_condition: Sharp HOUST declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.02 (i.e., signal direction reverses).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates, reflecting expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum. This signals improving economic fundamentals that support equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.7623 in October 2009, post-crisis recovery phase
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration does not precede positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates while unemployment remains elevated, indicating weak consumer demand despite labor market stabilization. This mismatch signals demand-side fragility that produces negative 20-day forward returns as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 705180 million dollars, UNRATE at 4.0% in November 2009
regime_hint: weak demand recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: The interaction signal (low RSAFS growth × elevated UNRATE) is followed by positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This produces negative 60-day forward equity returns as recession risk premiums widen.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.0% in November 2009, post-crisis stabilization
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth accelerates while housing starts remain stable, indicating broad-based demand expansion beyond residential construction. This signals sustainable economic momentum that produces positive 60-day forward returns through multiple demand channels.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5% AND HOUST month-over-month change between -5% and +5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22749.846 billion chained 2017 dollars in Q3 2009
regime_hint: broad-based demand expansion with housing stabilization
failure_condition: Broad demand expansion signals are followed by negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains at zero while payroll employment growth accelerates, indicating monetary accommodation supporting labor market recovery. This combination signals policy-supported demand expansion that produces positive 20-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33 bps, PAYEMS at 158543 thousand in November 2009
regime_hint: zero-bound policy with labor market recovery
failure_condition: The interaction of zero rates and payroll acceleration is followed by negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
